feedback: Strong post. Personal anchor (FinTech/Synchrony) lands the pattern recognition before the news. The structural fragments ('Configs that nobody owns. Crashes that get blamed instead of investigated.') read exactly right. Closing question is genuinely specific and earns its place.

dimension_notes.voice: pass — multiple fragments, 7+ first-person references, zero banned words, strong sentence variation, no em dashes
dimension_notes.framing: pass — reaction to one specific event cluster, genuine personal moment from FinTech career
dimension_notes.platform: pass — ~1523 chars (slightly over 1500 sweet spot, well under 2000 ceiling), hook works in first 210, specific closing question
dimension_notes.epistemic: pass — no hedges, assertive takes backed by specific sourced claims (HN score, IPO valuation, departure count)
dimension_notes.structure: pass — asymmetric paragraphs, fragments break monotony, no neat-bow closing
dimension_notes.framework: pass — Nut-graf structure: personal pattern recognition → news facts → analysis → personal stakes → question. Facts precede opinion throughout.
